title: liliaceae
intro: The Liliaceae rank, also known as the lily family, is a group of flowering plants that are typically perennial, often bulbous, and have six-petaled flowers. This family encompasses a wide range of plants, including lilies, hyacinths, and daylilies, which are commonly found in various habitats worldwide. The Liliaceae family is characterized by its unique characteristics, such as the presence of tepals and the formation of bulbous underground storage organs. With over 90 genera and more than 600 species, the Liliaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
